source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The pole-zero diagram of a causal and stable discrete-time system is shown in the figure. The zero at the origin has multiplicity 4. The impulse response of the system is \( h[n] \). If \( h[0] = 1 \), we can conclude:
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: \( h[n] \) is real for all \( n \)

choice_map.A: \( h[n] \) is real for all \( n \)
choice_map.B: \( h[n] \) is purely imaginary for all \( n \)
choice_map.C: \( h[n] \) is real for only even \( n \)
choice_map.D: \( h[n] \) is purely imaginary for only odd \( n \)

the conclusion about whether h[n] is real requires inference from pole-zero structure rather than direct lookup
the problem is multiple-choice, but the correct option depends on diagram interpretation plus signal-system reasoning
